message:
test(fr-conformance): live RDB corpus harvester for decode_rdb (br-frankenredis-8w1e / r73v)

Adds the load half of br-frankenredis-r73v ("RDB+AOF round-trip parity")
as a live-oracle conformance test. The test spawns the vendored upstream
redis-server, populates ten distinct shapes — each crafted to land on
a specific RDB type byte — issues a synchronous SAVE, reads dump.rdb
out of --dir, and pushes the bytes through fr_persist::decode_rdb.

Coverage matrix (RDB type tag → seeding strategy):

  * RDB_TYPE_STRING              (0)  raw + integer-encoded
  * RDB_TYPE_SET                 (2)  forced past listpack threshold
  * RDB_TYPE_HASH                (4)  forced past listpack threshold
  * RDB_TYPE_ZSET_2              (5)  forced past listpack threshold
  * RDB_TYPE_SET_INTSET         (11)
  * RDB_TYPE_HASH_LISTPACK      (16)
  * RDB_TYPE_ZSET_LISTPACK      (17)
  * RDB_TYPE_LIST_QUICKLIST_2   (18)
  * RDB_TYPE_SET_LISTPACK       (20)
  * RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS_3 (21) with a consumer group

Acceptance: decode_rdb returns Ok with one entry per seeded key and the
auxiliary fields parse without error. This is the minimal "load
upstream-produced .rdb cleanly" half of r73v step 1; the re-emit +
'redis-server --loadrdb' cross-impl gate stays tracked separately.

To reach the dump.rdb file the harness needs the spawned server's
working directory, so VendoredRedis grows a small data_dir() accessor
returning &Path to the existing _tmp_dir. No fields renamed, no Drop
behavior touched.

The new test is gated by the same skip_if_no_oracle() pattern as the
rest of the live harness, so CI without the vendored binary still
passes cleanly.

Untracked .beads/*.corrupt_orphan_recovery_* and
.beads/*.backup_orphan_recovery_* artifacts in the working tree are
intentionally NOT staged — those are ephemeral db_repair byproducts
(see hs8w in git history), not source-of-truth state.
